summary: Russian forces reported liberation of Zapselie and Riasnoe in Sumy region. Ukrainian infrastructure was hit, including a WFP warehouse in Dnipro destroyed by an Iskander missile. Numerous UAV movements were reported across multiple oblasts.
key_points: Russian Defense Ministry announced liberation of Zapselie and Riasnoe in Sumy region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30438).
A WFP warehouse in Dnipro was struck by an Iskander missile, destroying food supplies for 130,000 people (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Ukrainian forces reported systematic bombing of Kiev by Russian Foreign Ministry (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
Multiple UAVs observed heading west or toward specific towns in Poltava, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, and Kharkiv regions (various kpszsu posts).
Russian assets in Euroclear remain frozen despite Moscow arbitration court decision (Source: @tass_agency/377657).
EU plans to integrate air defence with Copernicus and Galileo satellites (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30449).
Russia holds 7,000 Ukrainian service members and 10,000‑20,000 Ukrainian civilians (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30454).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces claim liberation of two Sumy villages, while a WFP warehouse in Dnipro was destroyed by an Iskander strike, highlighting intensified targeting of logistics. Ukrainian reports note systematic bombing of Kiev and widespread UAV activity across several fronts. These developments suggest escalating pressure on both civilian infrastructure and frontline positions.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported an Iskander missile strike on the WFP warehouse in Dnipro, destroying food supplies intended for 130,000 people and valued at $1.4 million (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Ukrainian forces noted systematic bombing of Kiev, with a shortage of air‑defence missiles prompting a request for 5‑10 additional missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).

Multiple UAV sightings were reported: a UAV on the north of Poltava heading west; UAVs in central Sumy moving toward Konotop and Kroleve; UAVs in Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Kryvyi Rih (Source: @kpszsu/62878, @kpszsu/62880).
Ukrainian intelligence indicated that Russia holds 7,000 Ukrainian service members and between 10,000‑20,000 Ukrainian civilians with Russian passports (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30454).
The Russian Defense Ministry announced the liberation of the populated areas of Zapselie and Riasnoe in Sumy region after weeks‑long battles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30438).
Russia’s Foreign Ministry declared the start of systematic bombing of Kiev (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
Russian assets in Euroclear Bank remain frozen despite a Moscow arbitration court ruling, as Euroclear does not recognize EU jurisdiction (Source: @tass_agency/377657).
The EU intends to integrate its air‑defence systems with Copernicus and Galileo satellites to counter Russian threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30449).

On the Sumy front, Russian "North" Group forces advanced up to 700 m on twenty‑two sectors, with skirmishes in Zapselie, Ivolzhansk, Kondratovka, Pisarevka and surrounding settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
In the Kharkiv sector, Russian assault units advanced up to 900 m on eleven sectors, fighting in Grano, Karayichnoe and Okhrimovka villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
UAV activity was reported in Sumy region heading toward Konotop and Kroleve from the centre of the oblast (Source: @kpszsu/62878).
UAVs were observed in Kharkiv heading toward Bogoduhiv from the north and south (Source: @kpszsu/62904).
UAVs detected on the north of Poltava moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/62878).
A reactive UAV was reported north of Kyiv heading toward Krasyatych from the east (Source: @kpszsu/62901).
UAVs observed from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa region, course to Serhiyivka (Source: @kpszsu/62882).
UAV activity noted on the north‑east direction of tactical aviation (Source: @kpszsu/62890).
UAVs reported in Mykolaiv region heading north‑west from the south of the oblast (Source: @kpszsu/62899).
A UAV from Solonchaky observed over Mykolaiv heading toward Blahodatne (Source: @eRadarrua/93801).
UAVs in Dnipropetrovsk region moving toward Kryvyi Rih and three UAVs heading toward Kryvyi Rih from Sofiyivka (Source: @kpszsu/62880, @eRadarrua/93763).
UAVs observed near the border of Poltava and Dnipropetrovsk heading west/south‑west (Source: @kpszsu/62899).
Russian Foreign Ministry announced systematic bombing of Kiev, indicating a sustained air campaign (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
Ukrainian forces reported a shortage of air‑defence missiles, requesting 5‑10 additional missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
UAV sightings across multiple oblasts suggest increased low‑altitude reconnaissance and strike activity (various kpszsu and eRadarrua posts).
No specific aircraft losses or sorties were detailed in the provided messages.

key_developments: Russian Defense Ministry announced liberation of Zapselie and Riasnoe in Sumy region after weeks‑long battles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30438).
A Russian Iskander missile destroyed a WFP warehouse in Dnipro, eliminating food supplies for 130,000 people (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Ukrainian officials reported systematic bombing of Kiev by Russian forces, highlighting an escalation in air attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
Ukrainian forces reported a shortage of air‑defence missiles, requesting 5‑10 additional missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30430).
Multiple UAVs were observed across Poltava, Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Kyiv, and Mykolaiv regions, indicating widespread drone activity (Source: various kpszsu posts).
Russia claims to hold 7,000 Ukrainian service members and 10,000‑20,000 Ukrainian civilians (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30454).
EU plans to integrate air‑defence systems with Copernicus and Galileo satellites to counter Russian threats (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30449).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are consolidating gains in the Sumy region while intensifying strategic bombing of Kiev, suggesting a shift toward targeting command and logistics nodes. The widespread UAV activity across multiple fronts indicates increased reconnaissance and potential strike capabilities, complicating Ukrainian air‑defence efforts already strained by missile shortages. The destruction of the WFP warehouse underscores a deliberate targeting of humanitarian supply lines, raising the risk of civilian hardship and international condemnation.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are seeking additional air‑defence missiles and likely reallocating assets to protect critical infrastructure in Kiev and frontline areas. International partners, notably the EU, are planning to enhance air‑defence integration, which may mitigate some of the aerial threat. Humanitarian agencies may need to relocate supplies following the Dnipro warehouse attack.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued monitoring of UAV flight paths is essential to anticipate low‑altitude threats and protect key logistical hubs. The reported Russian casualty and prisoner figures suggest ongoing large‑scale detention operations, which could be leveraged in future negotiations. Asset freezes in Euroclear highlight financial pressure points that may influence Russian economic resilience.
